info.name: UOWN Leasing API Documentation (FULL API)
info.description: The **UOWN Leasing API** enables seamless integration with the UOWN financing platform, allowing merchants to automate customer application submissions, invoice processing, contract management, and status tracking. **Key Features:** - **Application Submission** – Submit customer applications and receive synchronous responses with approval status. - **Invoice Operations** – Send invoices and perform additional actions such as updates, cancellations, returns, and modifications. - **Contract Handling** – Finalize leases by triggering digital contract signing and initiating the funding process. - **Status Retrieval** – Query application status at any point to track progress or confirm state transitions. **How the API Works** 1. The merchant submits a **customer application** via `sendApplication`. 2. If needed, the merchant sends an **invoice** via `sendInvoice`. 3. The API returns **payment options** for the customer. 4. The customer completes payment and signs the **digital contract**. 5. The merchant requests **funding** via settleApplication. 6. The API provides **status updates** via getApplicationStatus. 📌 **Integration Type:** This guide covers the **Full API Integration**, where all actions are performed via API without requiring manual interaction with the Merchant Portal. 📌 **Important:** Before integrating, merchants must obtain authentication credentials from UOWN (details below). --- # **Authentication** To access the **UOWN Leasing API**, merchants must provide authentication credentials with each request. The API will handle the authentication automatically when these credentials are included. **How to Authenticate** For each request, include the following properties in the request body or headers: - **userName** → Your assigned username - **setupPassword** → Your authentication password - **merchantNumber** → Your unique merchant identifier **How to Obtain Credentials** Merchants must contact **UOWN** to request their **API access credentials**. These credentials are required before making any API calls. **Steps to Get Your Credentials:** - Reach out to the **UOWN Team**. - Provide your **merchant details** and integration use case. - Receive your userName, setupPassword, and merchantNumber. - Use these credentials in all API requests. 📌 **Important:** Credentials are **merchant-specific** and should be kept secure. To access the UOWN Sandbox or Production environments, your **egress IP addresses must be whitelisted**. Please contact UOWN Support with the following information: - **Merchant / Client Name** - **Environment** (Sandbox / Production) - **Access Type** (Office Access / VPN Access / Application Access) - **Static IP Addresses** to be whitelisted > If you're using a cloud provider, it's recommended to route traffic through a **NAT Gateway** to maintain consistent IPs. More info: [Azure NAT Gateway](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/ip-services/configure-public-ip-nat-gateway) --- # **Application Flow** The **UOWN Leasing API** provides two possible application workflows, depending on how the merchant wants to handle the invoice submission process. #### **Workflow 1: Sending the Complete Application (Including Invoice)** In this flow, the merchant submits the **entire customer application**, including invoice details, within a **single API call** to `sendApplication`. This ensures that the application goes through a **complete approval process** in one step. ###### **Process:** 1. The merchant sends the **customer’s application** along with the **invoice details** via `sendApplication (with cart)`. 2. UOWN processes the application. 3. Once approved, UOWN returns a **link** for the customer to finalize the lease. 4. The customer completes payment information and signs the lease agreement via the provided link. #### **Workflow 2: Sending the Invoice Separately** In this alternative flow, the merchant submits **only the application** without including the invoice. This enables a **pre-approval process**, where the customer is given a credit limit before making a purchase decision. ###### **Process:** 1. UOWN processes the application and returns an **approval message** with a **pre-approved amount**. 2. The merchant can now **guide the customer’s purchase** based on the approved credit. 3. Once the customer selects their items, the **merchant submits the invoice separately** via `sendInvoice`. 4. UOWN returns a **lease finalization link** for the customer to complete the lease. 5. The customer completes payment information and signs the lease agreement via the provided link. #### **Choosing the Right Flow** | **Flow Type** | **When to Use** | **Key Benefits** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Complete Application (**`sendApplication` **with invoice)** | When the customer has already selected their items | Faster approval and lease finalization | | **Pre-Approval (**`sendApplication`**without invoice)** | When you want to provide customers with a credit limit before purchasing | More flexibility in guiding customer purchases | #### Invoice Operations and Adjustments The `sendInvoice` endpoint supports more than just invoice submission. It is also used to perform various operations on an existing lease application, such as: - Submitting a new invoice - Canceling an invoice - Returning one or more invoice items - Modifying an existing invoice 📌 **For complete details on each operation**, refer to the `sendInvoice` section in the [API Endpoints](#api-endpoints) menu. --- ## **Finalizing the Lease Agreement** After the customer application is approved, the customer must complete the finalization process, which includes: - Entering payment information. - Reviewing and accepting the lease terms. - Digitally signing the contract. This process finalization can be done in **two ways**: #### **Via UOWN Portal** The customer is provided with a **UOWN-hosted link** in the success response of the lease submission. When the customer accesses this link, they are redirected to the **UOWN platform** to complete the process. ###### **Example of Lease Finalization Flow (UOWN Portal)** - Customers put payment information. - Customers review their lease details. - The customer decides whether to opt in to the protection plan. - Customers sign the digital contract. - The lease agreement is completed. #### **Embedded Page in Merchant’s Platform** UOWN provides the ability for merchants to **embed the lease finalization pages directly within their own platform**. This ensures a seamless user experience, allowing customers to complete the lease process **without leaving the merchant’s environment**. ###### **Example of Lease Finalization Flow (Embbeded Pages)** - Customers put payment information. - Customers review their lease details. - The customer decides whether to opt in to the protection plan. - Customers sign the digital contract. - The lease agreement is completed. --- ## **Lease Finalization Notifications** Once the customer **signs or declines** the lease agreement, UOWN provides **three ways** for the partner to receive status updates. #### **1 - URL Redirect** After the customer **signs or declines** the contract, they are redirected to the `returnUrl` provided by the merchant in the application request. 📌 **How It Works:** 1. The partner provides a **return URL** during the application request. 2. After the customer **signs or declines** the contract, they are redirected to this URL. 3. Two parameters are appended to the URL: - `event=completed` → If the customer **signs** the contract. - `event=cancelled` → If the customer **declines** the contract. - `ata` → The **unique application identifier (UUID)**. ###### **Example Redirect URL:** **{returnUrl}?event=completed&ata=892828a0-f766-4183-add7-781cbbc1ac83** #### **2 - Post Message from the Iframe** If the lease signing process is embedded within an **iframe**, UOWN sends a **postMessage** notification once the customer **completes the signing**. 📌 **How It Works:** 1. The customer completes the **lease signing process** inside the embedded iframe. 2. UOWN **sends a message** from the iframe to notify the merchant’s system. 3. The merchant listens for the event and updates their system accordingly. #### **3 - Webhook Notification** Partners can configure a **webhook endpoint** to receive automatic lease status updates. 📌 **How It Works:** 1. The partner provides a **webhook URL** to UOWN. 2. Once the lease is **signed**, UOWN sends a `POST` request to the webhook. 3. The partner’s system processes the notification and updates the lease status. 4. We can configure a webhook url and message based on the requirements of the merchant --- # **Additional Information** This section provides extra context and technical requirements to support a smooth integration process with the UOWN Leasing API. #### **Sandbox Testing Guidelines** The UOWN sandbox environment includes specific rules for simulating application approvals and lease limits. #### **Underwriting Test Behavior** When testing in the sandbox, the **last digit of the SSN** determines the underwriting result: - SSNs ending in `9` will result in a **denied** application (test failure). - SSNs ending in `0` through `8` will result in an **approved** application (test pass). > These test outcomes simulate system behavior and do not reflect actual credit decisions. #### **Lease Amount Requirement** - The **minimum lease amount** is **$250**. Applications below this amount will not be approved. --- # **API Endpoints** This section provides an overview of the available API endpoints in the UOWN Leasing API, detailing their purpose and functionality. #### **Summary of API Endpoints** | **Endpoint** | **Method** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `/sendApplication` | `POST` | Submits a leasing application. Can include or exclude invoice details. | | `/sendInvoice` | `POST` | Submits invoice details separately when not included in `sendApplication`. | | `/getApplicationStatus` | `POST` | Retrieves the current status of a submitted lease application. | | `/settleApplication` | `POST` | Finalizes a lease application, triggering the funding process. | ###### **📌 Overview** The `sendApplication` endpoint is responsible for submitting a **customer's application** to UOWN for credit evaluation. This request may **include or exclude invoice details**, depending on the partner's integration flow. - If the **invoice details are included** in the request, UOWN will **return a lease finalization link**. - If the **invoice is not included**, UOWN will **return a pre-approved amount**, allowing the partner to guide the customer’s purchase before finalizing the lease. ###### **📌 Request Behavior** - A **successful request** will return either a **pre-approval** or a **final lease agreement link**. - If the application **fails validation**, an error response with details about the missing or invalid fields will be returned. ###### **📝 Request Fields** The following table outlines the fields **accepted** in the `sendApplication` request: | **Field Description** | **JSON Tag** | **Required** | **Notes** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Web User ID | `userName` | ✅ Required | Username will be provided when setting up the sandbox. | | Web User Password | `setupPassword` | ✅ Required | Password will be provided when setting up the sandbox. | | Locale String | `localeString` | | Default: `en_US`. | | URL String | `returnUrl` | | Customer gets redirected to this URL after signing the contract. | | Merchant Number | `merchantNumber` | ✅ Required | Provided when setting up the sandbox. | | Unique Identifier | `uuid` | | A unique number generated by merchants for transaction differentiation. | | External Reference Id | `externalReferenceId` | optional | A unique application identifier from the merchant per application. | | Merchant’s Customer Code | `customerCode` | | Customer identifier. | | Main Applicant with a Co-Applicant | `individualJointIndicator` | | `I` = Individual, `J` = Joint (default: Individual unless Co-Applicant info is provided). | | Main Applicant First Name | `mainFirstName` | ✅ Required | | | Main Applicant Last Name | `mainLastName` | ✅ Required | | | Main Applicant Middle Initial | `mainMiddleInitial` | | | | Main Applicant Date of Birth | `mainDOB` | ✅ Required | Format: `MMDDYYYY`. | | Main Applicant Social Security Number | `mainSSN` | ✅ Required | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Address Line 1 | `mainAddress1` | ✅ Required | | | Main Applicant Address Line 2 | `mainAddress2` | | | | Main Applicant City | `mainCity` | ✅ Required | | | Main Applicant State | `mainStateOrProvince` | | | | Main Applicant Zip Code | `mainPostalCode` | ✅ Required | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Time at Current Address | `mainAtAddressFrom` | | Format: `MMYY`. | | Main Applicant Previous Address Line 1 | `mainPrevAddress1` | | | | Main Applicant Previous Address Line 2 | `mainPrevAddress2` | | | | Main Applicant Previous City | `mainPrevCity` | | | | Main Applicant Previous State | `mainPrevStateOrProvince` | | | | Main Applicant Previous Zip Code | `mainPrevPostalCode` | | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Duration at Previous Address | `mainAtPrevAddressFrom` | | Format: `MMYY`. | | Main Applicant Home Phone Number | `mainHomePhone` | | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Cell Phone | `mainCellPhone` | ✅ Required | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Housing Status | `mainHousingStatus` | | `O` = Buy/Own, `R` = Rent, `PR` = Parents/Relative, `OT` = Other. | | Main Applicant Monthly Housing Payment | `mainMonthlyHousingPayment` | | Two decimal places. | | Main Applicant Mortgage Balance | `mainMortgageBalance` | | Integer. | | Main Applicant Home Value | `mainHomeValue` | | Integer. | | Main Applicant if Checking Account Exists | `mainCheckingAccount` | | `Y` = Exist, `N` = Do not exist. | | Main Applicant if Savings Account Exists | `mainSavingsAccount` | | `Y` = Exist, `N` = Do not exist. | | Main Applicant Time of Bank Account | `mainBankAccountDuration` | | Various options (`LESS_THAN_30_DAYS`, `OVER_1_YEAR`, etc.). | | Main Applicant Banking Account Number | `mainBankAccountNumber` | | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Banking Routing Number | `mainBankRoutingNumber` | | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Banking Account Opened Date | `mainBankAccountOpenedDate` | | Format: `MMDDYYYY`. | | Main Applicant Banking Account Type | `mainBankAccountType` | | `"CHECKING"`, `"SAVINGS"`. | | Main Applicant Employment Status | `mainEmplStatus` | | Various options (`D` = Disability, `E` = Employed, etc.). | | Main Applicant Duration at Current Employment | `mainAtEmployerFrom` | | Format: `MMYY`. | | Main Applicant Employer Name | `mainEmployerName` | ✅ Required | | | Main Applicant Main Occupation | `mainOccupation` | | | | Main Applicant Employer Phone Number | `mainEmployerPhone` | | Digits only, no hyphens. | | Main Applicant Last Paid Date | `mainLastPayDate` | | Format: `MMDDYYYY`. | | Requested Loan Amount | `requestedLoanAmount` | | | | Main Applicant Next Paid Date | `mainNextPayDate` | Configured per merchant | Format: `MMDDYYYY` (Contact UOWN for details). | | Main Applicant Frequency of Pay | `mainPayFrequency` | Configured per merchant | Options: `"WEEKLY"`, `"BI_WEEKLY"`, `"SEMI_MONTHLY"`, `"MONTHLY"`. | | Main Applicant Monthly/Annual Income | `mainMonthlyIncome/mainAnnualIncome` | ✅ Required | Integer. One of them is required. | | Main Applicant Monthly Net Income | `mainMonthlyNetIncome` | | Integer. | | Main Applicant Email Address | `emailAddress` | ✅ Required | | | Main Applicant Application Language | `languagePreference` | | `E` = English, `S` = Spanish. | | Main Applicant Marital Status | `mainMaritalStatus` | | `M` = Married, `U` = Unmarried. | | Main Applicant Mother’s Maiden Name | `mainMotherMaidenName` | | | | Main Applicant Desired Pay Frequency | `desiredPaymentFrequency` | | Options: `"DAILY"`, `"WEEKLY"`, etc. | | Deposit Amount | `depositAmount` | | Decimal, up to 10 digits, 2 decimal places. | | Order Total | `orderTotal` | | Decimal. | | Merchandise Sub Total | `merchandiseSubtotal` | | Sum total before tax. | | Sales Tax | `salesTax` | | Decimal. | | Invoice Number | `invoiceNumber` | | Merchant’s invoice order number. | | Order Discount Amount | `discountAmount` | | Discount applied, 10 digits max. | | Delivery Charge | `deliveryCharge` | | Decimal. | | Installation Charge | `installationCharge` | | Decimal. | | Miscellaneous Fees | `miscellaneousFees` | | Decimal. | | IP Address | `ipAddress` | ✅ Required | | | Seon FingerprintText\* | `seonFingerprintText` | ✅ Required | Fraud protection attributes. | | Name of Salesperson | `salesPerson` | | | When submitting an application via `sendApplication`, merchants have the option to **include invoice items directly within the request payload**. If the cart/order details are available at this stage, they should be provided to **eliminate the need for a separate** **`sendInvoice`** **request**. If invoice items are included, the request payload must contain a `lineItem` array, where each object represents an individual product from the order. The fields inside each `lineItem` object follow the structure below: | **Field Description** | **JSON Tag** | **Required** | **Notes** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Item Line Number | `lineItemLineNumber` | ✅ Required | Order line number. | | Product Number | `lineItemProductNumber` | ✅ Required | Product / SKU number. | | Product Description | `lineItemProductDescription` | ✅ Required | Description of the product. | | Product Category | `lineItemProductCategory` | ✅ Required | Category that the product belongs to. | | Item Type | `lineItemType` | ✅ Required | `D` (debit/sale) or `C` (credit/return). | | Quantity Ordered | `lineItemQuantityOrdered` | ✅ Required | Quantity ordered. | | Unit Price | `lineItemUnitPrice` | ✅ Required | Selling price per unit. Must be 10 digits or less with 2 decimal places. | | Extended Price | `lineItemExtendedPrice` | ✅ Required | Total price (unit price × quantity). Must be 10 digits or less with 2 decimal places. | ##### **\*SEON Fingerprint Integration (Fraud Protection)** UOWN uses the [SEON platform](https://seon.io/) to enhance fraud protection through device fingerprinting. Merchants must implement SEON in their websites or mobile apps to provide the `seonFingerprintText` value required during the leasing application process. This fingerprinting enables UOWN to detect suspicious activity based on browser, device, and behavioral signals during underwriting. ###### 🔧 Integration Instructions: - **Websites**: Include the SEON JavaScript Agent in your tag or load it dynamically (e.g., on Login or Payment actions). Use the [CDN-hosted script](https://docs.seon.io/api-reference#javascript-agent-v5) to always fetch the latest version. Call `seon.config()` and `seon.getBase64Session()` to generate the encrypted fingerprint string. - **iOS Apps**: Integrate the SEON SDK via [CocoaPods](https://github.com/seontechnologies/seon-ios-sdk-public). Enable **Wi-Fi** and **location entitlements** as required. - **Android Apps**: Integrate the SEON SDK via [Gradle](https://github.com/seontechnologies/seon-android-sdk-public). Ensure **Wi-Fi** and **location entitlements** are enabled. 📌 **Output**: All platforms must produce a `seonFingerprintText` value that is passed to UOWN in the `sendApplication` request payload. --- ## Send Invoice `sendInvoice` The `sendInvoice` endpoint allows partners to send invoices for lease transactions. It is used not only to submit items for purchase but also to perform various operations on an existing application, such as: - **Submit an invoice**: Send line items to complete the lease purchase. - **Cancel an invoice**: Keep the approved lease but remove the invoice. - **Return an invoice**: Process a return of one or more line items. - **Modify an invoice**: Update an existing invoice with new information. ###### **Request Structure** The request payload should include the following fields: | **Field** | **JSON Tag** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Web User ID | `userName` | ✅ Required | Unique username assigned to the merchant. | | Web User Password | `setupPassword` | ✅ Required | Password provided when setting up the integration. | | Merchant Number | `merchantNumber` | ✅ Required | Unique identifier assigned to the merchant. | | Account Number | `accountNumber` | ✅ Required | The account number returned from `sendApplication` response. | | Invoice Number | `invoiceNumber` | Recommended | Unique invoice identifier for tracking purposes. | | Order Type | `orderType` | ✅ Required | Defines the operation type (`1` = Sale, `5` = Cancel Application, etc.). | | Order Total | `orderTotal` | ✅ Required | The total amount of the order. | | Selected Payment Frequency | `selectedPaymentFrequency` | Optional | Customer's payment frequency (e.g., `WEEKLY`, `BI_WEEKLY`). | | Line Items | `lineItem` | ✅ Required for sales | List of products included in the order. | ###### **Line Item Fields** Each invoice can contain multiple line items. Below is the structure for each item: | **Field** | **JSON Tag** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Item Line Number | `lineItemLineNumber` | ✅ Required | Order line number. | | Product Number | `lineItemProductNumber` | ✅ Required | SKU or product ID. | | Product Description | `lineItemProductDescription` | ✅ Required | Description of the product. | | Product Category | `lineItemProductCategory` | ✅ Required | Category that the product belongs to. | | Item Type | `lineItemType` | ✅ Required | `D` = Debit (sale), `C` = Credit (return). | | Quantity Ordered | `lineItemQuantityOrdered` | ✅ Required | The number of units purchased. | | Unit Price | `lineItemUnitPrice` | ✅ Required | Price per unit (up to 2 decimal places). | | Extended Price | `lineItemExtendedPrice` | ✅ Required | Total price after quantity multiplication. | | Serial Number | `lineItemSerialNumber` | Optional | Unique serial number of the product. | | Base Price | `lineItemBasePrice` | Optional | Base price before taxes or discounts. | | Tax Amount | `lineItemTaxAmount` | Optional | Amount of tax applied to this line item. | #### Submitting an Invoice The `sendInvoice` endpoint allows merchants to **submit an invoice** for a previously approved application. This is the standard flow for finalizing a lease transaction. ###### 📌 How It Works: 1. Set `orderType` to `"1"` to indicate a sales transaction. 2. Provide the `accountNumber` of the approved application. 3. Include the total amount of the invoice in `orderTotal`. 4. Add line items to specify the products being purchased. 5. The response will confirm whether the invoice submission was successful. ###### **📝 Request Example (Submit an Invoice)** ``` json { "userName": "{{userName}}", "setupPassword": "{{setupPassword}}", "merchantNumber": "{{merchantNumber}}", "accountNumber": "123456789", "orderType": "1", "invoiceNumber": "INV123456", "orderTotal": 1500.00, "selectedPaymentFrequency": "WEEKLY", "lineItem": [ { "lineItemLineNumber": "101", "lineItemSerialNumber": "SN12345678", "lineItemProductNumber": "SKU98765", "lineItemProductDescription": "Smart TV 55-inch 4K", "lineItemProductCategory": "Electronics", "lineItemType": "D", "lineItemQuantityOrdered": 1, "lineItemUnitPrice": 1200.00, "lineItemBasePrice": 1200.00, "lineItemTaxAmount": 0.00, "lineItemExtendedPrice": 1200.00 } ] } ``` #### Canceling an Invoice The `sendInvoice` endpoint allows merchants to **cancel an invoice** without canceling the approved lease application. This means that while the invoice is removed, the **approved credit remains available for the customer**. ###### 📌 How It Works: 1. Set `orderType` to `"5"` in the request. 2. Provide the `accountNumber` of the application to be canceled. 3. The `orderTotal` must be set to `"0.00"` since no purchase is being made. 4. The response will confirm whether the cancellation was successful. ###### **📝 Request Example (Cancel Application)** ``` json { "userName": "{{userName}}", "setupPassword": "{{setupPassword}}", "merchantNumber": "{{merchantNumber}}", "localeString": "en_US", "orderType": "5", "accountNumber": "7acf3003-3356-47f3-85a6-8b11d6d77620", "orderTotal": "0.00" } ``` #### Returning an Invoice (Processing a Return) The `sendInvoice` endpoint allows merchants to return one or more items from an invoice. This does not cancel the entire lease but adjusts the invoice by refunding specific items. ###### 📌 How It Works: 1. Provide the `accountNumber` of the application. 2. Include the `invoiceNumber` and the updated `orderTotal`, reflecting the new amount after the return. 3. Add line items to specify which products are being returned. 4. If all items in the invoice need to be returned: - Option A (Full Return): Leave the item list empty and set `orderTotal` to `$0.00`. 5. If only one or some items need to be returned: - Option B (Partial Return): For each returned item, set the line-item type to `C`. ###### **📝 Request Example (Return an Invoice)** ``` json { "userName": "{{userName}}", "setupPassword": "{{setupPassword}}", "merchantNumber": "{{merchantNumber}}", "localeString": "en_US", "orderType": "1", "accountNumber": "7acf3003-3356-47f3-85a6-8b11d6d77620", "invoiceNumber": "INV123456", "orderTotal": 300.00, "lineItem": [ { "lineItemLineNumber": "001", "lineItemProductNumber": "SKU67890", "lineItemProductDescription": "Wireless Headphones", "lineItemProductCategory": "Electronics", "lineItemType": "C", "lineItemQuantityOrdered": 1, "lineItemUnitPrice": 300.00, "lineItemExtendedPrice": 300.00 }, { "lineItemLineNumber": "002", "lineItemProductNumber": "SKU67891", "lineItemProductDescription": "Mouse", "lineItemProductCategory": "Electronics", "lineItemType": "D", "lineItemQuantityOrdered": 1, "lineItemUnitPrice": 300.00, "lineItemExtendedPrice": 300.00 } ] } ``` #### Modifying an Invoice (Update Existing Invoice) The `sendInvoice` endpoint allows merchants to **update an existing invoice** by modifying line items, changing amounts, or applying discounts. This is useful when corrections or adjustments need to be made to a previously submitted invoice. ###### 📌 How It Works: 1. Set `orderType` to `"1"` to indicate an **invoice modification**. 2. Provide the `accountNumber` of the application. 3. Include the `invoiceNumber` to identify which invoice is being modified. 4. Update the `orderTotal` to reflect the new total amount after modifications. 5. Modify `lineItem` as needed (add, update, or remove products). 6. Do not include items that have been canceled or returned. 7. Ensure that all calculated values (`orderTotal`, `merchandiseSubtotal`, etc.) match the updated list of items. ###### **📝 Request Example (Modify an Invoice)** ``` json { "userName": "{{userName}}", "setupPassword": "{{setupPassword}}", "merchantNumber": "{{merchantNumber}}", "localeString": "en_US", "orderType": "1", "accountNumber": "7acf3003-3356-47f3-85a6-8b11d6d77620", "invoiceNumber": "INV123456", "orderTotal": 1800.00, "lineItem": [ { "lineItemLineNumber": "001", "lineItemProductNumber": "SKU12345", "lineItemProductDescription": "Laptop", "lineItemProductCategory": "Electronics", "lineItemType": "D", "lineItemQuantityOrdered": 1, "lineItemUnitPrice": 1500.00, "lineItemExtendedPrice": 1500.00 }, { "lineItemLineNumber": "002", "lineItemProductNumber": "SKU67890", "lineItemProductDescription": "Wireless Mouse", "lineItemProductCategory": "Accessories", "lineItemType": "D", "lineItemQuantityOrdered": 1, "lineItemUnitPrice": 300.00, "lineItemExtendedPrice": 300.00 } ] } ``` --- ## **Checking Application Status** `getApplicationStatus` The `getApplicationStatus` endpoint allows merchants to retrieve the current status of a lease application. This can be used to verify whether an application has been approved, declined, or is still in process. ###### 📌 **How It Works:** 1. Send a `POST` request to the `getApplicationStatus` endpoint. 2. Provide your authentication credentials: `userName`, `setupPassword`, and `merchantNumber`. 3. Identify the application using the `accountNumber` provided in the response from `sendApplication`. 4. The API will return key information about the application status, decision, funding, and more. ###### 📌 **Required Field:** - `accountNumber` → Must be provided and uniquely identifies the application. --- ###### **📘 Response Fields** | **Field Description** | **JSON Tag** | **Notes** | | --- | --- | --- | | Fault Detail | `faults` | Boolean indicating that a fault occurred. Only populated if a fault occurred. | | Field Error 1 | `fieldInError1` | If a fault occurs, the name of the field that caused the error will be placed here. | | Field Error 2 | `fieldInError2` | | | Field Error 3 | `fieldInError3` | | | Field Error 4 | `fieldInError4` | | | Field Error 5 | `fieldInError5` | | | Error Description | `sorErrorDescription` | Descriptive text describing the error(s) that occurred. | | Transaction Status | `transactionStatus` | `E0` = not approved, `E1` = approved | | Transaction Status Message | `transactionMessage` | A message about the transaction. If an error occurs and there is no `faults` value, this message will be shown. | | Authorization Number | `authorizationNumber` | If `transactionStatus` = `E1` (approved), this will contain the provider’s authorization number. | | Provider's URL | `providerURL` | If needed, the URL to connect to a provider for this transaction. | | Provider's Account Number | `accountNumber` | Same account number provided in the request. | | Status of the Application | `applicationFound` | `true` = application exists, `false` = not found. | | Application Timestamp | `applicationCreatedTimestamp` | Timestamp of when the application was created. | | Main Applicant First Name | `customerFirstName` | | | Main Applicant Last Name | `customerLastName` | | | Application Unique ID | `appUuid` | | | Application Merchant Invoice Number | `merchantInvoiceNumber` | | | Application Status | `currentStatus` | e.g. `UW_APPROVED`, `UW_DENIED`, `DENIED`, `CANCELLED_DUP_SSN`, `CONTRACT_CREATED` | | Application Description of Status | `statusDescription` | e.g. `SIGNED`, `FUNDING`, `FUNDED` | | Notification of a Signed Lease | `hasSignedLease` | Boolean flag (`true` / `false`) | | Continuation Eligibility | `canContinue` | Indicates if the application can proceed to the next step | | Approved Amount | `approvedAmount` | Approved lease amount | | Open to Buy | `openToBuy` | Boolean flag | | Account Balance | `accountBalance` | Current balance of the lease account | | Last Payment Amount | `lastPayment` | Amount of the last payment made | | Last Payment Date | `lastPaymentDate` | Date of last payment | | Next Payment Due Date | `paymentDueDate` | Date of next scheduled payment | | Name of Merchant | `merchantName` | | | Merchant Program Name | `merchantProgramName` | | | Amount to Be Funded | `amountToBeFunded` | Total amount that will be funded to the merchant. | | Application Submitted | `applicationSubmitted` | Boolean indicating whether the application was successfully submitted. | | External Reference ID | `externalReferenceId` | Optional external identifier provided by the merchant. | | Fund Request Timestamp | `fundRequestDateTime` | Timestamp when the funding was requested. | | Funded Timestamp | `fundedDateTime` | Timestamp when the funding was completed. | The `settleApplication` endpoint is used to finalize a lease application after the customer has **signed the contract** and the **products have been delivered**. This triggers the funding process and completes the lease lifecycle on the UOWN platform. ###### 📌 **When to Use:** Only call this endpoint **after**: - The customer has signed the lease digitally - The merchant has delivered the merchandise --- ###### **📤 Request Fields** | **Field Description** | **JSON Tag** | **Required** | **Notes** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Web User ID | `userName` | ✅ Required | Provided when setting up the sandbox. | | Web User Password | `setupPassword` | ✅ Required | Provided when setting up the sandbox. | | Locale String | `localeString` | Optional | Default: `en_US` | | Merchant Number | `merchantNumber` | ✅ Required | Provided when setting up the sandbox (e.g. `OL90202-0001`). | | Account Number | `accountNumber` | ✅ Required | Account number assigned to the customer after application submission. | --- ###### **📥 Response Fields** | **Field Description** | **JSON Tag** | **Notes** | | --- | --- | --- | | Fault Detail | `faults` | Boolean indicating if a fault occurred. | | Field Error 1 | `fieldInError1` | Name of the field that caused the error. | | Field Error 2 | `fieldInError2` | | | Field Error 3 | `fieldInError3` | | | Field Error 4 | `fieldInError4` | | | Field Error 5 | `fieldInError5` | | | Error Description | `sorErrorDescription` | Descriptive text explaining the error(s). | | Transaction Status Message | `transactionMessage` | Message about the transaction or error. | | Provider’s Account Number | `accountNumber` | Matches the `accountNumber` used in the request. | | Authorization Number | `authorizationNumber` | Returned if transaction is successful (`E1`). | | Provider’s URL | `providerURL` | URL to connect to the provider if needed. | | Name of Merchant | `merchantName` | | | Customer’s First Name | `customerFirstName` | | | Customer’s Last Name | `customerLastName` | | | Transaction Status | `transactionStatus` | `A0` = Not settled, `A1` = Settled | | Amount | `amount` | Amount involved in the transaction | | Payment Detail List | `paymentDetailsList` | Detailed info about payments (if available) | --- ## **Add Lease** **`addLease`** The `addLease` endpoint allows merchants to create an **additional lease** using the customer's **remaining approval amount** from a previously funded lease. ### 📌 When to Use: - The original lease must be **funded**. - The customer must have made their **first payment** on time. - There must be **remaining approval amount** available. - The structure is identical to a `sendInvoice` request, but uses a different endpoint. --- ### **Request Structure** | **Field** | **JSON Tag** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Web User ID | `userName` | ✅ Required | Provided during setup | | Web User Password | `setupPassword` | ✅ Required | Provided during setup | | Merchant Number | `merchantNumber` | ✅ Required | Unique ID for the merchant | | Locale String | `localeString` | Optional | Default: `en_US` | | Customer Code | `customerCode` | ✅ Required | Merchant’s internal customer ID | | Account Number | `accountNumber` | ✅ Required | The existing funded lease account number | | Order Type | `orderType` | ✅ Required | Always `"1"` for add lease | | Invoice Number | `invoiceNumber` | ✅ Required | New unique invoice number | | Order Total | `orderTotal` | ✅ Required | Total lease amount for the new invoice | | Selected Payment Frequency | `selectedPaymentFrequency` | Optional | e.g. `WEEKLY`, `BI_WEEKLY` | | Line Items | `lineItem` | ✅ Required | List of products being added | --- ### **Notes:** - This call should follow the same rules as `sendInvoice`, including validations on item structure and totals. - It creates a new invoice under the same customer account.
